typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination "platform=macOS,arch=arm64" -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath .spm-cache -derivedDataPath .derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Cross-surface assertions are implemented via direct reads/writes to a single SwitcherProfileStore instance without instantiating Settings/Dashboard/Popover surfaces, so real cross-surface wiring and handoff regressions are not detected.
VAL-CROSS-009/004 launch chaining is not exercised through browser/CLI launch adapters; tests only assert activeProfileID changes and include hypothetical comments instead of launch invocations.
VAL-CROSS-008 error CTA routing is not validated through error-state trigger and CTA navigation assertions; current test path only checks persistence updates.
VAL-CROSS-006 startup/sync log secret-safety is not covered by log capture/redaction assertions; tests only inspect metadata JSON content.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a stricter evidence gate requiring explicit UI-surface interaction traces, launch-adapter invocation assertions, and startup/sync log redaction checks before allowing followedProcedure=true for cross-surface integration features.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of switcher-cross-surface-integration-hardening found store-centric tests and no direct UI/launch/log evidence despite this being an integration-hardening contract.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that validation-contract assertions specifying UI integration traces must be satisfied with UI-level or harnessed integration tests, not solely store-state tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Validation-contract entries VAL-CROSS-001/002/003/007/008/010 call for UI integration evidence, while reviewed coverage remained mostly state-store assertions.
